questions.q: Which famous queen of Hastinapura was born a princess of Gandhara?
questions.explanation: Gandhari took her very name from her homeland, the northwestern kingdom of Gandhara.

questions.q: Which prince of Gandhara became infamous as the master of dice at the Kuru court?
questions.explanation: Shakuni, brother of Gandhari, rolled the dice that stripped the Pandavas of their kingdom.

questions.q: In which part of the ancient subcontinent did the kingdom of Gandhara lie?
options: The far northwest beyond the five rivers
The eastern coast near the sea
The southern plateau beyond the Vindhyas
The central plains along the Yamuna
questions.explanation: Gandhara was a mountain realm in the far northwest, beyond the five rivers of the Punjab.

questions.q: By popular tradition, the name of ancient Gandhara survives in which modern city?
questions.explanation: Popular tradition links Gandhara's name to Kandahar in Afghanistan, though scholars debate the etymology.

questions.q: Who ruled Gandhara as the father of Gandhari and Shakuni?
questions.explanation: King Subala of Gandhara was father to both the blindfolded queen and the master of dice.

questions.q: Gandhara's princess Gandhari was given in marriage to which king of Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Gandhari was married to the blind king Dhritarashtra, and bound her own eyes for life.

questions.q: What does Shakuni's patronymic Saubala indicate?
questions.explanation: Saubala simply means son of Subala, the king of Gandhara.

questions.q: Why is Dhritarashtra's blindfolded queen known by the name Gandhari?
options: It was a title meaning blindfolded one
It marked her as a princess of Gandhara
questions.explanation: Like many epic queens, she was called after her homeland, the kingdom of Gandhara.

questions.q: On which side did the forces of Gandhara fight in the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Shakuni led Gandhara's warriors for Duryodhana and the Kauravas through all eighteen days.

questions.q: What lifelong practice did Gandhara's princess Gandhari adopt at her marriage?
options: Binding her eyes with a cloth
questions.explanation: Refusing to see a world her blind husband could not, Gandhari blindfolded herself for life.

questions.q: Which Pandava killed Shakuni of Gandhara on the eighteenth day of the war?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva slew Shakuni on the final day, exactly as he had vowed years before.

questions.q: Which son of Shakuni carried Duryodhana's insulting final message to the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: Uluka, son of Shakuni, was sent as envoy to taunt the Pandavas on the eve of battle.

questions.q: Beyond which famous river country was Gandhara said to lie?
options: The five rivers of the Punjab
The dry bed of the Saraswati
questions.explanation: Gandhara lay in the mountainous northwest beyond the five rivers of the Punjab.

questions.q: What was Shakuni of Gandhara's relationship to Duryodhana?
questions.explanation: As Gandhari's brother, Shakuni was Duryodhana's maternal uncle and constant counsellor.

questions.q: Why did Shakuni leave Gandhara to live in Hastinapura?
options: He stayed on with his sister Gandhari after her wedding
He was banished from Gandhara by his father
He came to study weaponry under Drona
He fled a rebellion in his own kingdom
questions.explanation: Shakuni accompanied his sister to Hastinapura at her marriage and remained at the Kuru court.

questions.q: Which sage granted Gandhari, daughter of Gandhara, her boon of a hundred sons?
questions.explanation: Pleased by her hospitality, Vyasa granted Gandhari the boon of a hundred sons.

questions.q: In popular retellings, what physical trait marked Shakuni, the prince of Gandhara?
questions.explanation: The limp is emphasized in later retellings; Vyasa's text dwells on his cunning rather than his gait.

questions.q: For what single purpose did Gandhari once remove her lifelong blindfold?
options: To see her newborn firstborn son
To watch the dice game unfold
To look upon the field of Kurukshetra
To pour the power of her gaze into Duryodhana
questions.explanation: In the widely told episode, her stored gaze made Duryodhana's body nearly invincible before the mace duel.

questions.q: Whom did Gandhari curse when all her hundred sons lay dead?
questions.explanation: Gandhari cursed Krishna that the Yadava clan would destroy itself in thirty-six years.

questions.q: In later folk tellings, from what were Shakuni's obedient dice said to be made?
options: The bones of his dead father
The knuckles of a slain demon
questions.explanation: The bone-dice story comes from later folk tradition and is not found in Vyasa's epic.

questions.q: In Vyasa's epic, what chiefly drove Shakuni's plots against the Pandavas?
options: Revenge for kin imprisoned by the Kurus
His own claim to the Hastinapura throne
Devotion to his nephew Duryodhana's ambitions
A personal feud with the sage Vidura
questions.explanation: Vyasa shows Shakuni serving Duryodhana's envy; the imprisonment-revenge motive belongs to later retellings.

questions.q: After the war, whose Ashvamedha campaign brought him to Gandhara to face Shakuni's son?
questions.explanation: Following the sacrificial horse, Arjuna defeated the young Gandhara king and spared him at his mother's plea.

questions.q: Which two brothers of Shakuni were slain by Arjuna at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Arjuna killed Subala's sons Vrishaka and Achala, deepening Shakuni's hatred mid-war.

questions.q: With what weapon did Sahadeva finally kill Shakuni of Gandhara?
options: A sword stroke in close combat
A spear hurled from his chariot
questions.explanation: In the Shalya Parva, Sahadeva severs Shakuni's head with a broad-headed arrow.

questions.q: When did Sahadeva swear his vow to slay Shakuni?
options: At the tournament of the princes
In the dice hall after Draupadi's humiliation
On the eve of the Kurukshetra war
During the year of disguise in Virata
questions.explanation: After the dice game and Draupadi's humiliation, Sahadeva vowed to kill the man of the dice.

questions.q: Besides her hundred sons, which daughter was born to Gandhara's princess Gandhari?
questions.explanation: The mass of flesh was divided into a hundred and one parts, the last becoming the daughter Dushala.

questions.q: What force from Gandhara did Shakuni still command in the war's final hours?
options: A wall of armored war elephants
questions.explanation: On the eighteenth day Shakuni harried the Pandavas with his remaining cavalry before Sahadeva cut him down.

questions.q: How did Gandhari, Gandhara's most famous daughter, meet her end?
options: By fasting unto death at court
questions.explanation: Gandhari died in a forest fire with Dhritarashtra and Kunti during their final retreat to the woods.

questions.q: Where was Gandhari standing when she pronounced her great curse?
options: In the dice hall of Hastinapura
On the corpse-strewn field of Kurukshetra
At the city gates of Dwarka
questions.explanation: Walking among her dead sons on the battlefield, Gandhari turned her grief upon Krishna.

questions.q: What doom did Gandhari's curse pronounce upon Krishna?
options: He would be reborn a hundred times
His discus would abandon him in battle
His capital would burn within a year
His clan would slay itself and he would die alone
questions.explanation: The curse ripened at Prabhasa, where the Yadavas slaughtered one another and Krishna died alone by a hunter's arrow.
